page: Microsoft Azure E8ds_v6 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the Edsv6 family. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz, with 4 physical cores and 2 threads per core. The CPU cache configuration includes 48 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and a large 260 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per core.

This server includes 472 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, allowing for high-performance disk attachments. It does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The underlying processor supports advanced instruction sets, including Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), which accelerate vector and matrix operations directly on the CPU.

Benchmark data shows strong single-threaded performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2166.0 and a gzip compression speed of 31.18 GB/sec. Multi-core performance is average, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 8311.0 and a stress-ng multi-core score of 7989.75. Memory bandwidth is measured at 621.06 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 67.01 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. In database operations, the server achieves a Passmark database score of 5261.32 and handles 11.41 million Redis SET operations per second.

The server exhibits a clear tradeoff in machine learning workloads. While it achieves a strong text generation speed of 268.75 tokens/sec on a small 135M parameter LLM, its performance drops to a weak 8.46 tokens/sec on a medium 7B model, and it is highly constrained on larger models due to the lack of dedicated GPU acceleration. Given its high memory density and local storage capacity, the E8ds_v6 is best suited for memory-intensive workloads, relational databases, caching layers like Redis, and moderate data processing tasks.
description: The Microsoft Azure E8ds_v6 is a memory-optimized server from the Edsv6 family, featuring 8 dedicated vCPUs and 64.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz, it includes 472 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage. The architecture provides advanced hardware extensions such as AVX-512 and AMX. Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core capabilities, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2166.0 and rapid single-threaded gzip compression. However, multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics remain in the average tier. While the server handles small-scale LLM inference efficiently, performance degrades significantly on larger models due to the absence of a GPU. This configuration is highly cost-efficient for memory-heavy workloads, database operations, and caching applications that benefit from a high memory-to-core ratio and local SSD storage.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E8ds_v6 is an Edsv6-family server featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB of RAM, and 472 GB of local storage, optimized for memory-intensive database and caching workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E8ds_v6 is an Edsv6 server with 8 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 472 GB local storage, designed for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: An x86_64 server featuring high memory density, local storage, and advanced Intel instruction sets for memory-intensive and database workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor at 4.2 GHz.
64.0 GB of RAM providing a high 8:1 memory-to-vCPU ratio.
472 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability.
Strong single-core performance in compression and text processing benchmarks.
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for hardware-accelerated vector operations.
Well-suited for relational databases, Redis caching, and memory-heavy applications.
